dangerous_commands.description: Deletes the entire root filesystem recursively.
Deletes your entire home directory recursively.
Deletes all files in current directory recursively.
Grants full read/write/execute access to all users — a serious security risk.
Writes raw data directly to a disk device — can corrupt or wipe drives.
Formats a filesystem — will permanently erase all data on the target partition.
Fork bomb detected — will crash your system by exhausting process resources.
Downloads and executes a remote script without inspection — very risky.
Downloads and executes a remote script without inspection — very risky.
Switches to full root shell — escalates all privileges.

nmap.suggestion: HTTP detected on port 80 → Run directory brute-force
HTTPS detected on port 443 → Run web vulnerability scan
SSH detected on port 22 → Try credential brute-force
FTP detected on port 21 → Check for anonymous login
MySQL detected on port 3306 → Try default credentials
SMB detected on port 445 → Enumerate shares

gobuster.suggestion: Admin panel found → Try default credentials or brute-force login
Login page found → Test for SQL injection
API endpoint found → Enumerate API methods and parameters

ffuf.suggestion: Live endpoints found → Test for LFI/RFI or SSRF vulnerabilities

nikto.suggestion: Vulnerabilities detected → Search for exploit on Exploit-DB

sqlmap.suggestion: Database found → Enumerate tables and dump credentials

nmap.description: Network Mapper — discovers hosts and services on a network.

flags.-sS: SYN scan (stealth) — half-open scan, less likely to be logged
flags.-sV: Version detection — probes open ports to determine service version
flags.-sC: Default scripts — runs NSE scripts equivalent to --script=default
flags.-A: Aggressive scan — enables OS detection, version detection, script scanning, traceroute
flags.-Pn: Skip host discovery — treat all hosts as online

nmap.risks: Unauthorized scanning may be illegal. Only scan systems you own or have written permission to test.

gobuster.description: Directory/file brute-forcer for web servers using wordlists.

flags.-x: File extensions to search for (e.g. php,html,txt)

gobuster.risks: Can trigger IDS/IPS alerts. High thread counts may crash unstable web servers.

ffuf.description: Fast web fuzzer for discovering hidden files, directories, parameters, and vhosts.
ffuf.risks: High-speed fuzzing may cause DoS on target. Always get authorization first.

nikto.description: Web server vulnerability scanner — checks for dangerous files, outdated software, misconfigurations.

flags.-Tuning: Scan tuning to focus on specific vulnerability types

nikto.risks: Very noisy — easily detected by IDS. Produces clear log entries on the target.

sqlmap.description: Automated SQL injection detection and exploitation tool.

flags.--forms: Automatically parse and test forms on the page

sqlmap.risks: Can cause data corruption or trigger WAF blocks. Never run against production databases without authorization.

hydra.description: Fast network login cracker supporting many protocols.

flags.-f: Stop after first valid credential found

hydra.risks: Account lockouts, IDS alerts. Brute-forcing without permission is illegal.

flags.-l: Long format — shows permissions, owner, size, date

flags.644: rw for owner, r for group/others — common for files
flags.755: rwx for owner, rx for group/others — common for executables
flags.777: Full rwx for owner, group, and others — avoid in production

chmod.risks: Setting 777 on sensitive files is a major security vulnerability.

ssh.description: Secure Shell — encrypted remote login protocol.

flags.-N: No remote command — useful for tunnels only

ssh.risks: Keep private keys secure. Never share or expose private key files.

netcat.description: Network utility for reading/writing data across TCP/UDP connections.

flags.-e: Execute program after connect (use with caution)

netcat.risks: -e flag creates a reverse shell if used carelessly. Handle with care.
